title: Thomsonaria verticillata
taxonomy: Thomsonaria verticillata is classified within the family Hydrozoa, which is a group of small freshwater or marine animals known as hydroids. This classification is due to its colonial, multicellular structure and the presence of stinging cells called cnidocytes. The family Hydrozoa is a part of the phylum Cnidaria, which also includes corals, jellyfish, and sea anemones. Within the phylum Cnidaria, the class Hydrozoa is further divided into several orders, and Thomsonaria verticillata belongs to one of these orders. The specific classification of Thomsonaria verticillata is further detailed in the scientific literature, but the general classification within Hydrozoa is well established.
